title: austrobaeckea columnaris
intro: Austrobaeckea columnaris, also known as the Australian finger lichen, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Asterinaceae and is native to Australia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antifungal and antibacterial activities that can help to combat a range of infections. Austrobaeckea columnaris has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including skin conditions and wounds, due to its ability to promote healing and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a powder or tea, or made into a salve or ointment to be applied topically to affected areas.
